name: lever reverse grip preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on the preacher pad, ensuring your elbows are fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine. Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up. Rest your upper arms on the preacher pad, ensuring your elbows are fully extended. Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps. Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your elbows. conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
